revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=王塚古墳| 所在地=富山市婦中町羽根3-1| 位置=| 形状=前方後方墳| 規模=全長約58m、後方部高さ7.6m| 被葬者=不明| 築造年代=古墳時代前期| 出土品=| 史跡指定=[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）[[1月15日]]（国の史跡）| 画像=}} '''王塚古墳'''（おうづかこふん）は、[[富山県]][[富山市]]に存在する[[古墳]]である。
== 概要 == 標高130mの羽根丘陵の尾根上<ref name="ze" />、史跡面積2,452m<sup>2</sup><ref name="men" />に立地している[[古墳時代]]前期に築かれた大型の[[前方後方墳]]<ref name="ze" />（全長58m、後方部高さ7.6mで、富山県内で4番目の規模<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm 王塚、千坊山遺跡群]（富山市、2023年10月8日閲覧）</ref>）で、[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）[[1月15日]]に国の史跡に指定された<ref name="a" />。東側には富山平野が一望できる<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 王塚古墳]（富山市の観光公式サイトTOYAMANET、2023年10月8日閲覧）</ref>。
*全長 - 58m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ 王塚・千坊山遺跡群]（とやまの文化遺産、2023年10月8日閲覧）</ref> *最大幅 - 26m<ref name="a" /> *高さ - 4m<ref name="a" /> *後方部幅 - 33m<ref name="a" /> *後方部高さ - 7.6m<ref name="a" />
== 周辺 == 勅使塚古墳や千坊山遺跡などの、[[弥生時代]]後期から古墳時代前期にかけての代表的な古墳や集落が所在している<ref name="ze" />。
== アクセス == *[[富山駅]]から[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[高山本線]]15分、[[速星駅]]から[[タクシー]]で10分<ref name="ze" />。 *{{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}}[[北陸自動車道]][[富山西インターチェンジ|富山西IC]]から車で10分（無料120台分の駐車場有）<ref name="ze" />。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[:Category:日本の古墳画像|日本の古墳画像一覧]] *[[中部地方の史跡一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おうつかこふん}} [[Category:富山県の古墳]] [[Category:富山県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:富山市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=王塚古墳| 所在地=富山市婦中町羽根3-1| 位置=| 形状=前方後方墳| 規模=全長約58m、後方部高さ7.6m| 被葬者=不明| 築造年代=古墳時代前期| 出土品=| 史跡指定=[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）[[1月15日]]（国の史跡）| 画像=}} '''王塚古墳'''（おうづかこふん）は、[[富山県]][[富山市]]に存在する[[古墳]]である。
== 概要 == 標高130mの羽根丘陵の尾根上<ref name="ze" />、史跡面積2,452m<sup>2</sup><ref name="men" />に立地している[[古墳時代]]前期に築かれた大型の[[前方後方墳]]<ref name="ze" />（全長58m、後方部高さ7.6mで、富山県内で4番目の規模<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm 王塚、千坊山遺跡群]（富山市、2023年10月8日閲覧）</ref>）で、[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）[[1月15日]]に国の史跡に<ref name="a" />、[[2004年]]（[[平成]]16年）[[11月19日]]に王塚古墳周辺の墳墓などが国の史跡にそれぞれ指定された<ref>『富山市史 編年史<下巻>』（2015年3月20日、富山市発行）683頁。</ref>。東側には富山平野が一望できる<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 王塚古墳]（富山市の観光公式サイトTOYAMANET、2023年10月8日閲覧）</ref>。
*全長 - 58m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ 王塚・千坊山遺跡群]（とやまの文化遺産、2023年10月8日閲覧）</ref> *最大幅 - 26m<ref name="a" /> *高さ - 4m<ref name="a" /> *後方部幅 - 33m<ref name="a" /> *後方部高さ - 7.6m<ref name="a" />
== 周辺 == 勅使塚古墳や千坊山遺跡などの、[[弥生時代]]後期から古墳時代前期にかけての代表的な古墳や集落が所在している<ref name="ze" />。
== アクセス == *[[富山駅]]から[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[高山本線]]15分、[[速星駅]]から[[タクシー]]で10分<ref name="ze" />。 *{{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}}[[北陸自動車道]][[富山西インターチェンジ|富山西IC]]から車で10分（無料120台分の駐車場有）<ref name="ze" />。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[:Category:日本の古墳画像|日本の古墳画像一覧]] *[[中部地方の史跡一覧]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Japanese kofun| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}} '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}},..."
revision.text:
{{Japanese kofun| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, [[Toyama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an elevation of 130m<ref name="ze" /> and covers a designated historic site area of 2,452m<sup>2</sup><ref name="men" />. It was constructed during the early [[Kofun period]] as a large [[rectangular keyhole-shaped mound]]<ref name="ze" />. Measuring 58m in total length with the rear section reaching a height of 7.6m, it ranks as the fourth largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Archaeological Sites] (Toyama City, retrieved October 8, 2023).</ref>. It was designated as a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948<ref name="a" />, and on November 19, 2004, additional tombs surrounding the Ōzuka Kofun were also designated as a National Historic Site<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological Volume (Part 2)'', published March 20, 2015, by Toyama City, p. 683.</ref>. The eastern side of the site offers a panoramic view of the Toyama Plain<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City official tourism site, TOYAMANET, retrieved October 8, 2023).</ref>.
Key dimensions: * Total length: 58m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Archaeological Sites] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, retrieved October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4m<ref name="a" /> * Rear section width: 33m<ref name="a" /> * Rear section height: 7.6m<ref name="a" />
== Surrounding Area == The region features other significant archaeological sites, including {{ill|Chokushizuka Kofun|ja|勅使塚古墳}} and the Senbōyama Archaeological Site, which span the late [[Yayoi period]] to the early [[Kofun period]]<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From [[Toyama Station]] on the [[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]'s [[Takayama Main Line]], it is a 15-minute ride to [[Hayahoshi Station]], followed by a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From the {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] [[Toyama-nishi Interchange|Toyama West IC]], it is a 10-minute drive. Free parking for up to 120 vehicles is available<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]] * [[:Category:Images of Japanese kofun|List of Japanese kofun images]] * [[List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region]]

== Access == * From [[Toyama Station]] on the [[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]'s [[Takayama Main Line]], it is a 15-minute ride to [[Hayahoshi Station]], followed by a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From the {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}} [[Hokuriku Expressway]] [[Toyama-nishi Interchange|Toyama West IC]], it is a 10-minute drive. '''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, [[Toyama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an elevation of 130m<ref name="ze" /> and covers a designated historic site area of 2,452m<sup>2</sup><ref name="men" />. It was constructed during the early [[Kofun period]] as a large [[rectangular keyhole-shaped mound]]<ref name="ze" />. Measuring 58m in total length with the rear section reaching a height of 7.6m, it ranks as the fourth largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Archaeological Sites] (Toyama City, retrieved October 8, 2023).</ref>. It was designated as a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948<ref name="a" />, and on November 19, 2004, additional tombs surrounding the Ōzuka Kofun were also designated as a National Historic Site<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological Volume (Part 2)'', published March 20, 2015, by Toyama City, p. 683.</ref>. The eastern side of the site offers a panoramic view of the Toyama Plain<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City official tourism site, TOYAMANET, retrieved October 8, 2023).</ref>.
Key dimensions: * Total length: 58m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Archaeological Sites] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, retrieved October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4m<ref name="a" /> * Rear section width: 33m<ref name="a" /> * Rear section height: 7.6m<ref name="a" />

{{Japanese kofun| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.

It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.

== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.

'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:images of kofun in Japan|images of Kofun in Japan]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

[[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Otsuka Kofun (Toyama)]][[ja:王塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:images of kofun in Japan|images of Kofun in Japan]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:images of kofun in Japan|images of Kofun in Japan]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:images of kofun in Japan|images of Kofun in Japan]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

[[Category:kk]] [[ja:王塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.

[[Category:kk]] [[ja:王塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Otsuka Kofun (Toyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuka Kofun (Toyama)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:images of kofun in Japan|images of Kofun in Japan]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. {{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:images of kofun in Japan|images of Kofun in Japan]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:images of kofun in Japan|images of Kofun in Japan]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length aqqroximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length aqqroximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding burial mounds and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length aqqroximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length aqqroximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
