{{Infobox ancient site| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:images of kofun in Japan|images of Kofun in Japan]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.comment: removed [[Category:Images of kofun in Japan]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| shape=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| scale=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| construction_period=Early Kofun Period| artifacts=| historic_site_designation=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Cultural Heritage, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama other_designation, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama other_designation, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was designated a National other_designation on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama other_designation, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was designated a National other_designation on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also designated as National other_designations<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of other_designations in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun Period| burial goods=| historic_site_other_designation=January 15, 1948 (National other_other_designation)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama other_other_designation, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also other_designation1 as National other_other_designations<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of other_other_designations in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama other_other_designation, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also other_designation1 as National other_other_designations<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of other_other_designations in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of other_designations in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun Period| burial goods=| historic_site_other_designation=January 15, 1948 (National other_designation)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_designation on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama other_designation, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_designation on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also other_designation1 as National other_designations<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of other_designations in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

[[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of other_designations in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| buried_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun Period| burial goods=| historic_site_other_designation=January 15, 1948 (National other_designation)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_designation on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama other_designation, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_designation on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also other_designation1 as National other_designations<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of other_designations in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama other_designation, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_designation on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also other_designation1 as National other_designations<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of other_designations in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun Period| burial goods=| historic_site_other_designation=January 15, 1948 (National other_designation)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_designation on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama other_designation, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_designation on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also other_designation1 as National other_designations<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of other_designations in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun built| burial goods=| historic_site_other_designation=January 15, 1948 (National other_designation)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_designation on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama other_designation, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_designation on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also other_designation1 as National other_designations<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of other_designations in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length [[Category:pp error]]approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun built| burial goods=| historic_site_other_designation=January 15, 1948 (National other_designation)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_designation on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama other_designation, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_designation on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also other_designation1 as National other_designations<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of other_designations in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>. [[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市|12=draft}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun built| burial goods=| historic_site_Historic Site=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線|12=draft}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅|12=draft}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道|12=draft}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
