'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線|12=draft}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅|12=draft}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道|12=draft}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線|12=draft}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅|12=draft}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道|12=draft}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=draft}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama, Toyama|ja|富山市|12=draft}}, Fuchū-machi Hane 3-1| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun built| burial goods=| historic_site_Historic Site=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Toyama City|ja|富山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|ja|富山駅|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|JR West Takayama Main Line|ja|高山本線|12=draft}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|ja|速星駅|12=draft}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|ja|北陸自動車道|12=draft}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * [[:Category:kk]] * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=draft}}

2 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:王塚古墳 (富山県) up to rev 97331380
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=王塚古墳| 所在地=富山市婦中町羽根3-1| 位置=| 形状=前方後方墳| 規模=全長約58m、後方部高さ7.6m| 被葬者=不明| 築造年代=古墳時代前期| 出土品=| 史跡指定=[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）[[1月15日]]（国の史跡）| 画像=}} '''王塚古墳'''（おうづかこふん）は、[[富山県]][[富山市]]に存在する[[古墳]]である。
== 概要 == 標高130mの羽根丘陵の尾根上<ref name="ze" />、史跡面積2,452m<sup>2</sup><ref name="men" />に立地している[[古墳時代]]前期に築かれた大型の[[前方後方墳]]<ref name="ze" />（全長58m、後方部高さ7.6mで、富山県内で4番目の規模<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm 王塚、千坊山遺跡群]（富山市、2023年10月8日閲覧）</ref>）で、[[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）[[1月15日]]に国の史跡に<ref name="a" />、[[2004年]]（[[平成]]16年）[[11月19日]]に王塚古墳周辺の墳墓などが国の史跡にそれぞれ指定された<ref>『富山市史 編年史<下巻>』（2015年3月20日、富山市発行）683頁。</ref>。東側には富山平野が一望できる<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 王塚古墳]（富山市の観光公式サイトTOYAMANET、2023年10月8日閲覧）</ref>。
*全長 - 58m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ 王塚・千坊山遺跡群]（とやまの文化遺産、2023年10月8日閲覧）</ref> *最大幅 - 26m<ref name="a" /> *高さ - 4m<ref name="a" /> *後方部幅 - 33m<ref name="a" /> *後方部高さ - 7.6m<ref name="a" />
== 周辺 == 勅使塚古墳や千坊山遺跡などの、[[弥生時代]]後期から古墳時代前期にかけての代表的な古墳や集落が所在している<ref name="ze" />。
== アクセス == *[[富山駅]]から[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[高山本線]]15分、[[速星駅]]から[[タクシー]]で10分<ref name="ze" />。 *{{Ja Exp Route Sign|E8}}[[北陸自動車道]][[富山西インターチェンジ|富山西IC]]から車で10分（無料120台分の駐車場有）<ref name="ze" />。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[:Category:日本の古墳画像|日本の古墳画像一覧]] *[[中部地方の史跡一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おうつかこふん}} [[Category:富山県の古墳]] [[Category:富山県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:富山市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama|qid=Q204266}}| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun built| burial goods=| historic_site_Historic Site=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=| part_of={{ill|Ōzuka-Senbōyama Sites|qid=Q60745207}}}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, built during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It Ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From [[:en:Toyama Station|Toyama Station]], take the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|JR West Takayama Main Line]] for 15 minutes to [[:en:Hayahoshi Station|Hayahoshi Station]], then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Nishi IC|qq=|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} on the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]], a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|qq=|1=List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367249}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
== Ref ere nce s ==

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama|WD=Q204266}}| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun built| burial goods=| historic_site_Historic Site=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=| part_of={{ill|Ōzuka-Senbōyama Sites|WD=Q60745207}}}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, built during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It Ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From [[:en:Toyama Station|Toyama Station]], take the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|JR West Takayama Main Line]] for 15 minutes to [[:en:Hayahoshi Station|Hayahoshi Station]], then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Nishi IC|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} on the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]], a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|1=List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367249}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama|WD=Q204266}}| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun built| burial goods=| historic_site_Historic Site=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=| part_of={{ill|Ōzuka-Senbōyama Sites|WD=Q60745207}}}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, built during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It Ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From [[:en:Toyama Station|Toyama Station]], take the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|JR West Takayama Main Line]] for 15 minutes to [[:en:Hayahoshi Station|Hayahoshi Station]], then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Nishi IC|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} on the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]], a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|1=List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367249}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama|WD=Q204266}}| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun built| burial goods=| historic_site_Historic Site=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=| part_of={{ill|Ōzuka-Senbōyama Sites|WD=Q60745207}}}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, built during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It Ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From [[:en:Toyama Station|Toyama Station]], take the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|JR West Takayama Main Line]] for 15 minutes to [[:en:Hayahoshi Station|Hayahoshi Station]], then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Nishi IC|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} on the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]], a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|1=List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367249}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama|WD=Q204266}}| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun built| burial goods=| historic_site_Historic Site=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=| part_of={{ill|Ōzuka-Senbōyama Sites|WD=Q60745207}}}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, built during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It Ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From [[:en:Toyama Station|Toyama Station]], take the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|JR West Takayama Main Line]] for 15 minutes to [[:en:Hayahoshi Station|Hayahoshi Station]], then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Nishi IC|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} on the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]], a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|1=List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367249}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama|WD=Q204266}}| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun built| burial goods=| historic_site_Historic Site=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=| part_of={{ill|Ōzuka-Senbōyama Sites|WD=Q60745207}}}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, built during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It Ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From [[:en:Toyama Station|Toyama Station]], take the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|JR West Takayama Main Line]] for 15 minutes to [[:en:Hayahoshi Station|Hayahoshi Station]], then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Nishi IC|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} on the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]], a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|1=List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367249}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama|WD=Q204266}}| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun built| burial goods=| historic_site_Historic Site=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=| part_of={{ill|Ōzuka-Senbōyama Sites|WD=Q60745207}}}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Toyama (city)|Toyama City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, built during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It Ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From [[:en:Toyama Station|Toyama Station]], take the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|JR West Takayama Main Line]] for 15 minutes to [[:en:Hayahoshi Station|Hayahoshi Station]], then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Nishi IC|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} on the [[:en:Hokuriku Expressway|Hokuriku Expressway]], a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|1=List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367249}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:王塚古墳 (富山県)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Ōzuka Kofun| location={{ill|Toyama|WD=Q204266}}| position=| type=Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound| map_size=Total length approximately 58m; rear section height 7.6m| Interred_individual=Unknown| built=Early Kofun built| burial goods=| historic_site_Historic Site=January 15, 1948 (National Historic Site)| image=| part_of={{ill|Ōzuka-Senbōyama Sites|WD=Q60745207}}}}
'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama (city)|en|Toyama (city)|WD=Q204266|lt=Toyama City}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It Ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|en|Toyama Station|WD=Q1053070|lt=Toyama Station}}, take the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=JR West Takayama Main Line}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|en|Hayahoshi Station|WD=Q5685963|lt=Hayahoshi Station}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Nishi IC|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|en|Hokuriku Expressway|WD=Q1549512|lt=Hokuriku Expressway}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|1=List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367249}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|en|Toyama Station|WD=Q1053070|lt=Toyama Station|1=Toyama Station}}, take the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=JR West Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|en|Hayahoshi Station|WD=Q5685963|lt=Hayahoshi Station|1=Hayahoshi Station}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Nishi IC|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|en|Hokuriku Expressway|WD=Q1549512|lt=Hokuriku Expressway|1=Hokuriku Expressway}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|1=Q11367249|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367249}}

'''Ōzuka Kofun''' (王塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toyama (city)|en|Toyama (city)|WD=Q204266|lt=Toyama City|1=Toyama (city)}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped mound)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, built during the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948.
== Overview == The Ōzuka Kofun is situated on a ridge of the Hane Hills at an altitude of 130 meters<ref name="ze" />, covering a historic site area of 2,452 square meters<ref name="men" />. It is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 58 meters in total length with the rear mound standing 7.6 meters high. It Ranks as the fourth-largest kofun in Toyama Prefecture<ref name="men">[https://www.city.toyama.toyama.jp/etc/maibun/center/topics/ohduka_senbouyama/ohduka_senbouyama.htm Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama City, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>. The tumulus overlooks the Toyama Plain to the east<ref name="ze">[https://www.toyamashi-kankoukyoukai.jp/?tid=101831 Ōzuka Kofun] (Toyama City Tourism Official Website TOYAMANET, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref>.
* Total length: 58 m<ref name="a">[https://toyama-bunkaisan.jp/search/1628/ Ōzuka and Senbōyama Ruins] (Toyama Historic Site, accessed October 8, 2023).</ref> * Maximum width: 26 m<ref name="a" /> * Height: 4 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound width: 33 m<ref name="a" /> * Rear mound height: 7.6 m<ref name="a" />
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on January 15, 1948, and in 2004, the surrounding Kofuns and graves were also Historic Site1 as National Historic Sites<ref>''Toyama City History: Chronological History (Volume II)'' (published March 20, 2015, Toyama City), p. 683.</ref>.
== Surroundings == The area includes other representative kofun and settlements from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to the early Kofun period, such as the Chokushizuka Kofun and Senbōyama Ruins<ref name="ze" />.
== Access == * From {{ill|Toyama Station|en|Toyama Station|WD=Q1053070|lt=Toyama Station|1=Toyama Station}}, take the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=JR West Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}} for 15 minutes to {{ill|Hayahoshi Station|en|Hayahoshi Station|WD=Q5685963|lt=Hayahoshi Station|1=Hayahoshi Station}}, then a 10-minute taxi ride<ref name="ze" />. * From {{ill|Toyama Nishi IC|ja|富山西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Nishi IC|1=Toyama-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10954161}} on the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|en|Hokuriku Expressway|WD=Q1549512|lt=Hokuriku Expressway|1=Hokuriku Expressway}}, a 10-minute drive (free parking available for 120 cars)<ref name="ze" />. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|ja|中部地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in the Chūbu region|1=Q11367249|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367249}}
